topic: In China employee termination: role-relay legal governance model for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination governance for foreign employers
related_keywords: foreign employer labor dispute risk China

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal framework for lawful termination routes, compensation basis, and unlawful termination consequences.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Operational interpretation layer needed for role handoffs and documentation requirements.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Provides dispute-stage perspective and informs evidence/audit-trail standards.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Supports payroll and statutory contribution closure governance during offboarding.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Best-fit practical source for foreign-reader translation of legal routes into operating controls.

source_replacement_notes: Kept legal-hierarchy core unchanged because this run is governance-architecture-led rather than tax-calculation-led.
Replaced matrix-centric framing from prior run with role-relay governance framing while retaining the same high-confidence legal base.
Excluded generic comparative commentary to reduce interpretation drift and keep China-specific source authority high.
consensus: Foreign employers should classify legal route before assigning operational tasks.
Dispute risk is strongly influenced by evidence quality and cross-function consistency.
Governance failures usually occur at handoff points between legal, HR, manager, payroll, and vendors.
uncertainties: City-level adjudication practice and evidentiary emphasis can vary.
Specific treatment details for complex payment components may need local confirmation.
English-language official texts can lag nuanced Chinese-language implementation updates.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for a role-relay governance article because the source set combines binding law, implementation regulation, dispute interpretation, and one foreign-employer-facing practice translator.
